ddls:
CREATE TABLE "universities" (
  "university_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each university */,
  "university_name" TEXT /* Name of the university */,
  "national_rank" INTEGER /* National rank of the university */,
  "world_rank" INTEGER /* Global rank of the university */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the university is located */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the university is located */,
  "established_year" INTEGER /* Year the university was established */,
  "website" TEXT /* Official website of the university */,
  "logo_url" TEXT /* URL of the university's logo */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the university */,
  "total_students" INTEGER /* Total number of students */,
  "international_students" INTEGER /* Number of international students */,
  "faculty_staff_ratio" REAL /* Ratio of faculty to staff */,
  "campus_size" INTEGER /* Size of the campus in square meters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("university_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rankings" (
  "ranking_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ranking entry */,
  "university_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the university */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the ranking */,
  "total_score" REAL /* Total score of the university */,
  "alumni_score" REAL /* Score for alumni success */,
  "award_score" REAL /* Score for awards received */,
  "hici_score" REAL /* Score for high citation index */,
  "ns_score" REAL /* Score for normalized score */,
  "pub_score" REAL /* Score for publications */,
  "pcp_score" REAL /* Score for academic reputation */,
  "research_score" REAL /* Score for research output */,
  "academic_reputation_score" REAL /* Score for academic reputation */,
  "employer_reputation_score" REAL /* Score for employer reputation */,
  "international_faculty_score" REAL /* Score for international faculty */,
  "international_student_score" REAL /* Score for international student presence */,
  "citations_per_faculty_score" REAL /* Score for citations per faculty */,
  "total_students" INTEGER /* Total number of students */,
  "total_faculty" INTEGER /* Total number of faculty */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ranking_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rankings_university_id FOREIGN KEY ("university_id") REFERENCES universities ("university_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rankings_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric */,
  "weightage" REAL /* Weightage of the metric in the overall ranking */,
  "formula" TEXT /* Formula used to calculate the metric score */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "national_rankings" (
  "national_ranking_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each national ranking entry */,
  "university_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the university */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the national ranking */,
  "national_rank" INTEGER /* National rank of the university */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the national ranking (e.g., public, private, research) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("national_ranking_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_national_rankings_university_id FOREIGN KEY ("university_id") REFERENCES universities ("university_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "world_rankings" (
  "world_ranking_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each world ranking entry */,
  "university_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the university */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the world ranking */,
  "world_rank" INTEGER /* World rank of the university */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the world ranking (e.g., public, private, research) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("world_ranking_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_world_rankings_university_id FOREIGN KEY ("university_id") REFERENCES universities ("university_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data entry, analyst, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time of access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who generated the report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "generation_time" TEXT /* Time when the report was generated */,
  "parameters" TEXT /* Parameters used for generating the report */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path of the generated report */,
  "file_size" INTEGER /* Size of the generated report file */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., generated, pending, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report access entry */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the report was accessed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user who accessed the report */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_access_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metric_scores" (
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each score entry */,
  "ranking_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the ranking entry */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the metric */,
  "score" REAL /* Score value for the metric */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("score_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metric_scores_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metric_scores_ranking_id FOREIGN KEY ("ranking_id") REFERENCES rankings ("ranking_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metric_scores_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data source was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "source_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  "university_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the university */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the data */,
  "data_value" TEXT /* Value of the data collected */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of the data (e.g., numerical, text) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_source_data_university_id FOREIGN KEY ("university_id") REFERENCES universities ("university_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_source_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "source_metrics" (
  "source_metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source metric entry */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the metric */,
  "weightage" REAL /* Weightage of the metric in the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_source_metrics_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_source_metrics_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)
